rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purposes Of Posting The Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] California law allows for an oral deposition to be taken by remote means. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.310(a), "At the election of the deponent or the deposing party, the deposition officer may attend the deposition at a different location than the deponent via remote means. A deponent is not required to be physically present with the deposition officer when being sworn in at the time of the deposition." Pursuant to California Rules of Court, rule 3.1010(a)(1), "Any party may take an oral deposition by telephone, videoconference, or other remote electronic means, provided: (1) Notice is served with the notice of deposition or the subpoena[.]" Rule 3.1010 was adopted to establish implementing procedures for Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.310. In other words, the Discovery Act does not require the deponent party and the attorney conducting the deposition to participate from the same location. An oral deposition may be taken "by telephone, videoconference, or other remote electronic means," so long as the oral deposition is properly noticed as such under the statute. If any of the parties choose to take oral deposition, of each other or of a nonparty, that oral deposition shall be noticed for EITHER (1) remote appearance over videoconference, or (2) as a written deposition conducted pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 2025.330(e), 2028 through 2028.080. NEITHER PARTY MAY NOTICE AN IN-PERSONAL ORAL DEPOSITION, OF EACH OTHER OR OF A NONPARTY WITNESS. Any oral deposition conducted over videoconference under this Order shall be recorded by the deposition officer, in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 2025.340. The deposition officer may appear remotely, at their election. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling issued by Judge Michael B. McNaughton. Before the court is Respondents' and Trustees', Krista M. Brugnara and Eric J. Brugnara, Motion for Protective Order ("Motion"). Per the Notice of Motion, Movants seek a protective order pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 2025.420, 2025.410, 2025.310-2025.340,2025.220, 2025.230, and 2025.250, 2025.270, 1987.1, 2017.010, and 2023.010-2023.030, providing various forms of relief regarding oral depositions in this matter, specifically (1) that all depositions in this proceeding, both party and nonparty, regardless of which party notices the deposition, be taken only upon written questions pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure sections 2025.310-2025.340; (2) that all oral depositions be stayed and restrained absent prior Court order; and (3) the Court impose cost-shifting and advance-payment conditions. No opposition to the Motion was filed. Movants' Request for Judicial Notice of certain court orders is GRANTED, pursuant to Evidence Code section 452. The court notes, there is no declaration regarding Movants' attempts to meet and confer in accordance with Code of Civil procedure section 2016.040 prior to filing the Motion (see, Code of Civ. Proc. sec. 2025.420(a).) The court infers from counsel's declaration in support of the Motion that such efforts would be futile, based on prior communications with Petitioner. A party seeking a protective order bears the burden of establishing good cause for such an order. (See, e.g. Nativi v. Deutsche Bank National Trust Co. (2014) Cal.App.4th 261, 318.) The court, for good cause shown, may make any order that justice requires to protect any party, deponent, or other natural person or organization from unwarranted annoyance, embarrassment, or oppression, or undue burden and expense. (Code of Civil Procedure sec. 2025.420). The Motion is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART, as follows. On the basis of the factual statements and argument made in the Motion, there is no discovery propounded by either party currently pending, nor has any oral deposition been noticed in this matter. The Motion's request that all oral depositions be stayed and allowed only by court order is DENIED without prejudice, as premature. The Motion's request for cost-shifting is DENIED without prejudice, as premature. If any of the parties choose to propound written discovery, on each other or on a non-party witness, the parties must propound Code-compliant written discovery in accordance with the Civil Discovery Act (Code of Civil Procedure secs. 2016 to 2061). Meet and confer efforts shall be over email or letter only.
